original_prompt: Please create a new file named 'hawaii-packing-list.docx' containing the packing list for the vacation plans in Hawaii based on the other document. Then, share the document with john.doe@gmail.com with read permissions
assessment_reasoning: The request includes almost all necessary details: exact filename, recipient email, and permission level. The only ambiguity is "the other document" — it doesn't explicitly identify which existing file or topic to use as the source, nor specify the exact content/format beyond “packing list,” so the agent must search/assume which document to base the list on and how to format the items. Otherwise the task and constraints are clear.

variations.L1: Make a packing list for my Hawaii trip and share it with John.
variations.L2: Create a packing list for my Hawaii vacation based on the other document, save it as hawaii-packing-list (a document file), and share it with john.doe@gmail.com.
variations.L3: Please create a new file named 'hawaii-packing-list.docx' containing the packing list for the vacation plans in Hawaii based on the other document. Then share that document with john.doe@gmail.com.
variations.L4: Please create a new file named 'hawaii-packing-list.docx' containing the packing list for the vacation plans in Hawaii based on the other document. Then, share the document with john.doe@gmail.com and grant read-only permissions.
variations.L5: Follow these exact steps: (1) run search_files to find the Hawaii vacation plans/source document (use the topic/query for that document); (2) extract the packing list items from that document; (3) call create_file with filename='hawaii-packing-list.docx' and content formatted as a bullet list (each item on its own line starting with '- '); (4) call share_file with file_id equal to the newly created file's id, email='john.doe@gmail.com', and permission='r' (read-only). Return confirmation and the new file id when done.
